How It Operates Internally: Opens real payment channels automatically using a Lightning Service Provider (LSP). Incorporates efficient technologies like Taproot channels and splicing. Uses trampoline routing (pathfinding delegated to LSP).
Ideal For: Daily payments while maintaining full control of your keys with zero technical friction.
Can Send/Receive on L1 (On-Chain)?: Yes (via Splice-In / Splice-Out — requires on-chain confirmation)
Can Send/Receive Ecash (Cashu/Fedimint)?: No (Can pay Ecash invoices via LN translator, but cannot hold Ecash tokens)

How It Operates Internally: Does not manage channels for you: acts as a 'remote control' to connect directly to your own home node (Umbrel, RaspiBlitz, etc.). Performs source-based routing using a full network graph — no LSP dependency.
Ideal For: Advanced users and sovereign node operators who manage their own liquidity.
Can Send/Receive Ecash (Cashu/Fedimint)?: No (Can pay Ecash invoices via LN, cannot hold tokens)

How It Operates Internally: Runs a lightweight embedded Lightning node on the device with LSP assistance for channel management and liquidity. Similar to Category 1 but with more user control.
Ideal For: Users who want own-node sovereignty with the convenience of LSP-assisted liquidity.
Can Send/Receive Ecash (Cashu/Fedimint)?: No (Can pay Ecash invoices via LN)

Technology Category: 3. Hybrid On-Chain / Swaps (L1 with LN Connector)
How It Operates Internally: Your funds are always stored on-chain as UTXOs in a 2-of-2 multisig (device key + server key). When paying on Lightning, performs a Submarine Swap using a Swap Provider. For small amounts, fees are optimized via batched settlement. Neither party can move funds unilaterally. Emergency Kit allows full recovery without servers.
Ideal For: Users who prefer a unified on-chain balance and use Lightning occasionally.
Can Send/Receive on L2 (Lightning)?: Yes (via Submarine Swap — fees vary with congestion)
Can Send/Receive Ecash (Cashu/Fedimint)?: No (Can pay Ecash invoices via LN swap)

How It Operates Internally: Exchanges Bitcoin for cryptographic IOUs (Ecash tokens) using blind signatures (BDHKE). The Mint holds the backing Bitcoin collateral and runs its own Lightning node. Tokens are bearer instruments stored locally — unlinkable to any transaction. Fedimint distributes trust across a federation of Guardians.
Ideal For: Mathematical privacy, absolute micro-payments, instant same-mint transfers with near-zero fees.
Can Send/Receive on L1 (On-Chain)?: No (Generally — some Mints support on-chain deposits, not standard)
Can Send/Receive on L2 (Lightning)?: Yes (via Mint's LN node — Mint pays/receives invoices)
Can Send/Receive Ecash (Cashu/Fedimint)?: Yes (Native within same Mint — no Lightning needed. Cross-Mint requires LN)

How It Operates Internally: A company manages all nodes, channels, and keys on its own servers. In your app you only see a balance reflection (an internal IOU in their database). Intra-company transfers are pure database updates — no blockchain or Lightning involved.
Main Wallets: Wallet of Satoshi, Strike, Satoshi Tango
Ideal For: Absolute beginners who want to experience Lightning speed without configuring anything.
Can Send/Receive Ecash (Cashu/Fedimint)?: No (Can pay Ecash invoices via company's LN, cannot hold tokens)
